mnemonics: In-Place Reversal: Flip the Links, Don't Make a Copy
keyPoints: Iterate through the list, reversing the 'next' pointers of each node.
Maintain three pointers: previous, current, and next to track nodes during reversal.
The head of the original list becomes the tail of the reversed list.
commonMistakes: Losing reference to the next node before updating pointers, which breaks the list.
Forgetting to update the head pointer to point to the new first node (previously the last node).
Not handling the empty list (head is null) or single-node list edge cases correctly.
